visible_observations: A mature curvy woman with long braided hair pinned into a crown-like arrangement stands in fitted black trousers, heeled boots, a sleeveless plum wrap top, and a wide strapped belt.

Copper-colored cracks or script-like lines spread across her face, neck, chest, and arms; she extends one open hand toward the viewer and carries a rectangular handled board marked with circular diagrams in the other.
The braided pin crown, copper skin network, board, direct gaze, and stable wide stance create a clear controlled-caster presentation.
defects: The open inviting hand, relaxed stance, slight smile, and carried diagram board read more as demonstration or recruitment than immediate combat threat.
The fitted dark trousers, boots, belts, sleeveless wrap, and carried occult reference object follow a familiar dark-caster costume and prop pattern.
The copper lines could be decorative tattoos or jewelry-like surface design because the body shows no visible pain, deformation, or functional consequence.
The rectangular board explains study or planning but does not visibly function as weapon, defense, or active supernatural mechanism.
uncertainties: The image alone does not establish whether the copper network is injury, corruption, power, inscription, or ornament.
The exact intent of the extended hand is unresolved; it could invite, command, cast, bargain, or simply present.
The board's diagrams are too small to reveal whether it is a tool, shield, portable map, or decorative identifier.

visible_observations: Three labeled candidates show the same pinned braided hairstyle, fitted wrap outfit, trousers, boots, and rectangular diagram board with an open palm, a raised finger while hugging the board, and an extended stop-like palm.
The poses alter arm reach, board placement, head direction, and stance width while preserving the same overall body and costume.
defects: The exploration changes gesture and board position but not body plan, garment family, prop class, or core silhouette.
Fine hair pins and braid strands provide distinct detail but are thin perimeter elements vulnerable to loss at reduced size.
Each candidate carries the same rectangular board, so the prop repeatedly supplies the main asymmetry rather than a distinct combat action.
The rendered interior lines make this less legible as a pure outer-contour comparison.
uncertainties: The A, B, and C labels do not explicitly state which candidate was selected.
The candidate gestures could be casting, command, refusal, or conversation; no target or effect resolves their action.

visible_observations: The head tilts slightly while the eyes look directly toward the viewer beneath mildly asymmetrical brows; the lips form a small closed smile.
Braids and pins frame a conventionally attractive mature face, and copper crack-like lines cross the forehead, cheeks, neck, chest, and visible arm.
defects: The head tilt, raised brow, soft eyes, and small smile read primarily as confident friendliness, knowing amusement, or invitation.
There is little visible jaw, eyelid, mouth, or cheek tension to expose fear, compulsion, pain, hunger, or concealed conflict.
The face appears unaffected by the extensive copper skin network, which makes that network feel decorative rather than costly.
The extended hand enters the crop but carries no visible target, effect, or resistance that clarifies the smile's intent.
uncertainties: Unaided viewing cannot distinguish seduction, manipulation, reassurance, command, or ordinary confidence.
It is unclear whether the copper lines sit on the skin, crack through it, or glow beneath it.

visible_observations: At native size, the long dark hair mass, extended arm, narrow waist, wide hips, separated legs, and hanging rectangular board remain visible.
The open hand and board create the strongest silhouette asymmetry.
defects: The face, copper skin network, braid pins, garment construction, board diagrams, and hand expression collapse at this size.
The dark trousers, boots, hair, and background merge through much of the lower and rear body.
The surviving outline reads as a generic board-carrying caster, with little of the copper-cost or facial identity retained.
uncertainties: No neighboring unit icons, gameplay background, selection frame, or motion state are shown, so in-context recognition and gesture meaning cannot be determined.
